
Recruitment {data-navmenu="Population"}
=====================================

Row {.tabset}
-------------------------------------

### Recruitment Trend

```{r rec_trend}
mv_plot_trend(
  df         = mv$recruitment,
  ylab       = "Recruitment",
  colour     = "#2ca02c",
  title      = "Recruitment",
  log_buttons = TRUE
)
```

### Recruitment Deviations

```{r rec_devs, eval=!is.null(mv$rec_devs)}
rd <- mv$rec_devs
p <- plot_ly(data = rd, x = ~year, y = ~est)
p <- add_bars(
  p = p,
  marker = list(
    color = ifelse(
      test = rd$est >= 0,
      yes  = "#2ca02c",
      no   = "#d62728"
    )
  ),
  showlegend = FALSE,
  hovertemplate = "%{x}: %{y:.3f}<extra></extra>"
)
lay <- modelVis:::.mv_layout()
lay$xaxis$title <- "Year"
lay$yaxis$title <- "Recruitment deviation"
lay$title <- "Recruitment Deviations"
lay$shapes <- list(list(
  type = "line",
  x0 = min(rd$year), x1 = max(rd$year),
  y0 = 0, y1 = 0,
  line = list(color = "#666666", dash = "dash")
))
p <- do.call(what = layout, args = c(list(p = p), lay))
modelVis:::.mv_config(p = p)
```

### Stock-Recruitment

```{r sr_plot, eval=!is.null(mv$stock_recruit)}
mv_plot_sr(
  df    = mv$stock_recruit,
  title = "Stock-Recruitment Relationship"
)
```
